title: Off-chain session token from verified handshake

artifacts.description: Session token specification: JWT/SD-JWT claim schema, trust anchor (txHash), nullifier binding, scope encoding, expiry policy, and revocation semantics.

issueSessionToken(verifiedHandshake, signingKey, opts): produces a signed SD-JWT encoding nullifierHash, humanMerkleRoot, agentPermissions bitmap, txHash, iat, exp. Also exports verifySessionToken(token, verifyingKey) for downstream fast-path auth.
SessionTokenPayload interface: nullifierHash, humanMerkleRoot, agentPermissions, txHash, sessionNonce, iat, exp, iss. Extend VerifyHandshakeResult to carry txHash.
Python mirror: issue_session_token() and verify_session_token() calling the JS subprocess bridge. Thin wrapper — no re-implementation of crypto.
Unit tests: happy-path issuance after mock verifyHandshake, tampered-payload rejection, expired token rejection, permission-bitmap round-trip, txHash claim presence.
Python-side tests: issue → verify round-trip via subprocess bridge, expired token, missing txHash.
Usage guide: how to issue after verifyHandshake, present to downstream tool calls, validate latency profile, token lifetime recommendations, and revocation via nullifier registry.

steps: Define SessionTokenPayload schema in spec/session-token-bolyra.md: mandatory claims (nullifierHash, humanMerkleRoot, agentPermissions, txHash, sessionNonce, iat, exp, iss), optional SD-JWT disclosures for individual permission bits.
Extend VerifyHandshakeResult in sdk/src/types.ts to include txHash (on-chain transaction hash returned by the verifier contract call).
Implement issueSessionToken() in sdk/src/session.ts using jose (ES256 or EdDSA): pack payload, sign with operator signing key, return compact SD-JWT string.
Implement verifySessionToken() in sdk/src/session.ts: verify signature, check exp, assert txHash non-empty, decode agentPermissions bitmap, return typed SessionTokenPayload.
Add selective-disclosure layer: wrap agentPermissions individual bits as SD-JWT disclosures so downstream tools receive only the scope they need, not the full bitmap.
Wire issueSessionToken into the public SDK API (sdk/src/index.ts exports) and update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a two-line example.
Implement Python bridge in sdk-python/bolyra/session.py: call JS SDK via existing subprocess bridge pattern, parse returned JSON, expose typed dataclass.
Write TypeScript tests (sdk/test/session.test.ts): happy path, tampered payload, expired, wrong key, missing txHash, permission bitmap round-trip.
Write Python tests (sdk-python/tests/test_session.py): round-trip via subprocess, error propagation.
Run full test suite (npm test + pytest -v), fix any failures, then verify sub-100ms cold-path verification with a quick benchmark in the test output.
Add docs/session-tokens.md: issuance flow diagram, lifetime guidance (recommend ≤15 min for agentic sessions), revocation note (nullifier blacklist on verifier contract).
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md to reference the session token as the fast-path auth layer in Section 5 (Protocol Flow).
dependencies: verifyHandshake() must return the on-chain txHash from the verifier contract call
jose library (npm) for JWT/SD-JWT signing — already a common peer dep, confirm it is present or add to sdk/package.json
sd-jwt-vc or @sd-jwt/core for selective disclosure layer (evaluate against jose's built-in SD-JWT support in jose >=5.x)
Operator signing key management: caller must supply an ES256 or EdDSA private key; spec must define key registration / rotation semantics
On-chain nullifier registry (contracts/) must be queryable for revocation checks — tie to existing BolyraRegistry if present
Python subprocess bridge (sdk-python/bolyra/_bridge.py or equivalent) must be stable before Python session module is added
